title: paeonia veitchii var. woodwardii
intro: Paeonia veitchii var. woodwardii, also known as Wood's peony or the Yunnan peony, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the peony family (Paeoniaceae) native to the Himalayas and southwestern China. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Paeonia veitchii var. woodwardii include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's root and rhizome are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are also used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
